events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess structural framing residential, missing jack studs, tested/inspected. Compare against IRC R502, R602. Missing jack studs means header openings not properly supported; jack studs (trimmer studs) are required to support header and transfer loads to bottom plate/sill. Non-compliant, installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Missing jack studs can cause header failure, structural collapse over openings. Residential framing critical but maybe high. Need choose. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Work stage tested/inspected. Percent complete maybe 100 for inspected stage? Could be 90. Value 0. Confidence 0.95. Findings: missing jack studs at openings; header loads not transferred to supporting studs; non-compliant with IRC. Actions: install jack studs per code/drawing; inspect all openings; repair any damaged framing. Rationale. References: IRC R602.7? Actually headers require jack studs/trimmers. R602.7 Jack studs or framing anchors. R502 floor? For floor openings. R602 wall framing. We'll include IRC R602.7, R502.7 maybe. Need exact? We can say IRC R602.7 (headers and jack studs), IRC R502.7 (floor opening framing). Since prompt says apply rather than quote mechanically.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Jack studs are missing at framed openings.",
    "Header loads are not properly transferred to the supporting wall framing.",
    "Framing does not comply with prescriptive code requirements for openings."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install required jack studs (trimmers) at each side of every structural opening per the approved plans and code.",
    "Verify header size, bearing length, and fastening for all openings.",
    "Re-inspect all openings before closing or covering the work."
  ],
  "rationale": "IRC wall and floor framing provisions require jack studs to support headers at openings. Missing jack studs creates a load-path defect that can lead to header failure and structural instability, so the work fails inspection.",
  "references": [
    "IRC R602.7 - Headers, jack studs, and framing at wall openings",
    "IRC R502.7 - Floor opening framing and support"
  ]
}
